---
title: "Project 1"
author: "Lu Bai"
date: "2026-02-25"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(cowplot)

library(posterior)
library(bayestestR)
library(mcmcse)
library(loo)

library(tableone)
library(flextable)

library(broom)
library(extras) # exp10()
library(brms)
```


# Data Preparation
```{r}
# Read in data
hiv_data <- read.csv("~/Downloads/hiv_6624_final.csv")
hiv_data$log_vload <- log10(hiv_data$VLOAD)

hiv_clean <- hiv_data %>%
  # Handle missing/improbable values
  mutate(
    log_vload = ifelse(log_vload < 0.5, NA, log_vload),
    BMI = ifelse(BMI > 90 | BMI < 0, NA, BMI),
    AGG_MENT = ifelse(AGG_MENT < 2, NA, AGG_MENT),
    AGG_PHYS = ifelse(AGG_PHYS < 2, NA, AGG_PHYS),
    LEU3N = ifelse(LEU3N < 50, NA, LEU3N),
    RACE = factor(
      ifelse(RACE %in% c(2,3,4,7,8), 0, RACE),
      levels = c(0,1),
      labels = c("Other", "Non-hispanic White")),
    EDUCBAS = factor(
      ifelse(EDUCBAS %in% c(1,2,3,4), 0, 1),
      levels = c(0,1),
      labels = c("Not finish college and lower", "College degree and higher")
  ))
```

```{r}
# -------------------------
# Baseline (Year 0)
# -------------------------
baseline <- hiv_clean %>%
  filter(years == 0) %>%
  select(newid,
         hard_drugs,   # baseline exposure
         age, # baseline age
         BMI,
         RACE,
         EDUCBAS,
         SMOKE,
         log_vload,
         LEU3N,
         AGG_PHYS,
         AGG_MENT) %>%
  distinct(newid, .keep_all = TRUE)

# -------------------------
# Year 2 Outcomes
# -------------------------
year2 <- hiv_clean %>%
  filter(years == 2) %>%
  select(newid,
         ADH,
         log_vload,
         LEU3N,
         AGG_PHYS,
         AGG_MENT) %>%
  distinct(newid, .keep_all = TRUE)

# -------------------------
# Merge
# -------------------------
hiv_merge <- baseline %>%
  inner_join(year2, by = "newid")
```

# Descriptive Table 

```{r}
vars <- c("age", "BMI", "RACE", "EDUCBAS", "SMOKE", "ADH",
          "log_vload.x", "LEU3N.x", "AGG_PHYS.x", "AGG_MENT.x",
          "log_vload.y", "LEU3N.y", "AGG_PHYS.y", "AGG_MENT.y" )

# Define categorical variables
factorVars <- c("RACE", "EDUCBAS",
                "SMOKE", "ADH")

# Create the table1
# We stratify by Drug use
table1 <- CreateTableOne(
  vars = vars,
  strata = "hard_drugs",
  data = hiv_merge,
  factorVars = factorVars,
  addOverall = TRUE
)

# Generate table1 with missing data information
tab_df <- print(table1, 
      missing = TRUE, 
      printToggle = FALSE,
      noSpaces = TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Variable") %>%
  select(-c(test,p))
  
# Rename the variable
tab_df$Variable <- c("N", "Age at visit, mean (SD)", "BMI, mean (SD)",
                     "Race = White, non-Hispanic, n(%)",
                     
                     "Baseline Education = College degree and higher, n(%)",
                     
                     "Smoking Status, n(%)", "  Never smoked", "  Former smoker", "  Current smoker",
                     
                     "Adherence to meds taken, n(%)", "  100%", "  95-99%", "  75-94%", "  <75%",

                     "Baseline Log10 viral load (copies/ml), mean (SD)", 
                     "Baseline number of CD4 positive cells, mean (SD)",
                     "Baseline aggregate physical QOL score, mean (SD)", 
                     "Baseline aggregate mental QOL score, mean (SD)",
                     
                     "Year 2 Log10 viral load (copies/ml), mean (SD)", 
                     "Year 2 number of CD4 positive cells, mean (SD)",
                     "Year 2 aggregate physical QOL score, mean (SD)", 
                     "Year 2 aggregate mental QOL score, mean (SD)"
                     )

colnames(tab_df) <- c("Variable", "Overall", 
                      "No hard drug use", "Hard drug used",
                      "Rate of Missing (%)")

# Render the table
ft <- flextable(tab_df)

ft <- ft %>%
  autofit() %>%
  align(j = 2:5, align = "center", part = "body") %>%
  align(j = 1, align = "left", part = c("body")
) %>%
  bold(part = "header") %>%
  set_caption("Table 1. Descriptive statistics of baseline demographic characteristics and treatment response at year 2 by hard drug use")

ft
```

# Distribution of outcomes

```{r}
# Viral load
p_vload <- ggplot(hiv_clean, aes(x = VLOAD)) +
  geom_density(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Viral Load (Raw)",
       x = "Viral Load",
       y = "Density")

p_logv <- ggplot(hiv_merge, aes(x = log_vload.y)) +
  geom_density(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Viral Load (log scale)",
       x = "log(Viral Load)",
       y = "Density")

# CD4 count
p_cd4 <- ggplot(hiv_merge, aes(x = LEU3N.y)) +
  geom_density(fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of CD4 Count",
       x = "CD4 Count",
       y = "Density")

p_phys <- ggplot(hiv_merge, aes(x = AGG_PHYS.y)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "orange", alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
  labs(title = "Physical quality of life(QOL) score Distribution",
       x = "SF36 PCS score",
       y = "Count")

p_ment <- ggplot(hiv_merge, aes(x = AGG_MENT.y)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "darkred", alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  labs(title = "Mental QOL Distribution",
       x = "SF36 MCS score",
       y = "Count")

# Combine plots
p_combine <- plot_grid(p_vload, p_logv, p_cd4, p_phys, p_ment, ncol = 2, labels=c('A1', 'A2', 'B', 'C', 'D'))
title <- ggdraw() + draw_label("Figure A1. Density Plots of Outcomes", fontface='bold')
plot_grid(title, p_combine, ncol=1, rel_heights=c(0.1, 1)) # Adjust rel_heights

```

# Non-Bayesian Modeling

## Data Management
```{r}
hiv_merge <- hiv_merge %>%
  mutate(
    SMOKE = factor(
      ifelse(SMOKE %in% c(1,2), 0, 1),
      levels = c(0,1),
      labels = c("Not current smoker", "Current smoker")),
    ADH = factor(
      ifelse(ADH %in% c(2,3,4), 0, 1),
      levels = c(0,1),
      labels = c("<100%", "100%")
  )) %>%
  filter(
    !if_any(ends_with(".y"), is.na)
  )

# Write complete case data frame to a CSV file
#hiv_merge <- na.omit(hiv_merge)
#write.csv(hiv_merge, file = "~/Downloads/hiv_merge.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

## Fit non-Bayesian models
```{r}
# linear models
# ---------------------------------------------------------
freq_vload <- lm(log_vload.y ~ hard_drugs + log_vload.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE, 
                       data = hiv_merge)

freq_CD4 <- lm(LEU3N.y ~ hard_drugs + LEU3N.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE, 
                       data = hiv_merge)

freq_phys <- lm(AGG_PHYS.y ~ hard_drugs + AGG_PHYS.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE, 
                       data = hiv_merge)

freq_ment <- lm(AGG_MENT.y ~ hard_drugs + AGG_MENT.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE, 
                       data = hiv_merge)
```

```{r}
# Extract results
extract_results <- function(model, outcome, exp = FALSE) {

  tidy_model <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)

  hard_row <- tidy_model %>%
    filter(term == "hard_drugs")

  if (exp == TRUE) {
    hard_row <- hard_row %>%
      mutate(
        estimate = exp10(estimate),
        conf.low = exp10(conf.low),
        conf.high = exp10(conf.high)
      )
  }

  hard_row %>%
    transmute(
      Outcome = outcome,
      Estimate = round(estimate,3),
      SD = round(std.error,3),
      `95% CI` = paste0("(", round(conf.low, 3), ", ",
                  round(conf.high, 3), ")"),
      `p value` = round(p.value,3)
    )
}

# Combine into one table
results_table <- bind_rows(
  extract_results(freq_vload, "Viral Load (Exp10)", exp = TRUE),
  extract_results(freq_CD4, "CD4 Count"),
  extract_results(freq_phys, "Physical QOL"),
  extract_results(freq_ment, "Mental QOL")
)

# Create flextable
ft_freq <- flextable(results_table)

ft_freq <- ft_freq %>%
  set_caption("Table 2. Adjusted Estimates of Baseline Hard Drug Use on Year 2 Treatment Responses") %>%
  colformat_num(j = c("Estimate", "SD"), digits = 3) %>%
  autofit() %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  align(align = "center", part = "all")

ft_freq
```

# Bayesian Modeling

```{r}
# BAYESIAN MODELS
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# brms uses Stan for MCMC sampling

## Viral load
bayes_vload <- brm(
  formula = log_vload.y ~ hard_drugs + log_vload.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE,
  data = hiv_merge,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "b"),      # Coefficients
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "sigma", lb = 0)    # Error term
  ),
  chains = 4, iter = 10000, warmup = 2000, core = 4, seed = 123
)

## CD4 count
bayes_CD4 <- brm(
  formula = LEU3N.y ~ hard_drugs + LEU3N.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE,
  data = hiv_merge,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "b"),      # Coefficients
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "sigma", lb = 0)    # Error term
  ),
  chains = 4, iter = 10000, warmup = 2000, core = 4, seed = 123
)

## Physical QOL
bayes_phys <- brm(
  formula = AGG_PHYS.y ~ hard_drugs + AGG_PHYS.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE,
  data = hiv_merge,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "b"),      # Coefficients
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "sigma", lb = 0)    # Error term
  ),
  chains = 4, iter = 10000, warmup = 2000, core = 4, seed = 123
)

## Mental QOL
bayes_ment <- brm(
  formula = AGG_MENT.y ~ hard_drugs + AGG_MENT.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE,
  data = hiv_merge,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "b"),      # Coefficients
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "sigma", lb = 0)    # Error term
  ),
  chains = 4, iter = 10000, warmup = 2000, core = 4, seed = 123
)
```

```{r}
extract_bayes_results <- function(model, outcome_name, 
                                  direction = "positive",
                                  clinically_meaningful = 0, exp = FALSE) {
  
  # Get posterior samples for probability calculations
  draws <- as_draws_df(model)
  beta <- draws$b_hard_drugs
  
  # Posterior summary for hard_drugs
  summ <- as.data.frame(posterior_summary(model, variable = "b_hard_drugs"))
  
  # 95% HPDI (Highest Posterior Density Interval)
  hpdi_95 <- bayestestR::hdi(beta, .ci = 0.95)
  
  # Handle Back-transformation if the outcome was log10-transformed
  # Transform the Mean, and the Confidence Intervals
  if (exp == TRUE) {
    summ <- summ %>%
      mutate(
        Estimate = exp10(Estimate))
    
    hpdi_95$CI_low <- 10^as.numeric(hpdi_95$CI_low)
    hpdi_95$CI_high <- 10^as.numeric(hpdi_95$CI_high)
  }
  
  # Probability of Direction (pd)
  pd <- max(mean(beta > 0), mean(beta < 0))
  
  # Probability in expected direction
  if(direction == "positive"){
    prob_clinical  <- mean(beta > clinically_meaningful)
  } else {
    prob_clinical  <- mean(beta < -clinically_meaningful)
  }
  
  tibble(
    Outcome = outcome_name,
    Estimate = round(summ$Estimate, 3),
    SD = round(summ$Est.Error, 3),
    CI_Lower = round(hpdi_95$CI_low, 3),
    CI_Upper = round(hpdi_95$CI_high, 3),
    pd = round(pd, 3),
    Prob_Clin_Meaningful = round(prob_clinical, 3)
  )
}

bayes_results_table <- bind_rows(
  
  # Viral load: harm = increase
  # clinically_meaningful stays in 0.2 log units is approx a 1.58x increase)
  extract_bayes_results(bayes_vload, "Viral Load (Exp10)",
                        direction = "positive",
                        clinically_meaningful = 0.2,
                        exp = TRUE),
  
  # CD4: harm = decrease
  extract_bayes_results(bayes_CD4, "CD4 Count",
                        direction = "negative",
                        clinically_meaningful = 50),
  
  # Physical QOL: harm = decrease
  extract_bayes_results(bayes_phys, "Physical QOL",
                        direction = "negative",
                        clinically_meaningful = 2),
  
  # Mental QOL: harm = decrease
  extract_bayes_results(bayes_ment, "Mental QOL",
                        direction = "negative",
                        clinically_meaningful = 2)
)

# Render with Flextable
ft_bayes <- flextable(bayes_results_table) %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Outcome = "Outcome",
    Estimate = "Estimate",
    SD = "Posterior SD",
    CI_Lower = "2.5% HDPI",
    CI_Upper = "97.5% HDPI",
    pd = "p value",
    Prob_Clin_Meaningful = "Pr(Clinically Meaningful Harm)"
  ) %>%
  set_caption("Table 3. Bayesian Estimates and Posterior Probabilities of Harm from Hard Drug Use Across Treatment Outcomes") %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  autofit()

ft_bayes
```

# Diagnostics

## Figures

```{r}
# DIAGNOSTICS & COMPARISON
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Define a function to create a combined diagnostic row for a single model
get_diagnostic_row <- function(model, label) {
  draws <- as_draws_array(model)
  params <- c("b_hard_drugs", "sigma")
  
  # 1. Trace Plot: inspect chains' mixing and stationary: chains overlapping, no trends, good mixing
  p1 <- mcmc_trace(draws, pars = params) + 
    ggtitle(paste(label, ": Trace")) + 
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"), # Title size
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),               # X and Y label size
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),                # X and Y tick labels
    legend.position = "none")
  
  # 2. Autocorrelation (ACF) Plots: drops quickly toward 0
  p2 <- mcmc_acf(draws, pars = params) + 
    ggtitle(paste(label, ": ACF")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"), # Title size
    axis.title = element_text(size = 5),               # X and Y label size
    axis.text = element_text(size = 5),                # X and Y tick labels
    legend.position = "none")
  
  # 3. Posterior Density plots: all chains densities overlap closely
  p3 <- mcmc_dens_overlay(draws, pars = params) + 
    ggtitle(paste(label, ": Posterior Density")) + 
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"), # Title size
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),               # X and Y label size
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "none")
  
  # Combine into a horizontal row
  return(grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, nrow = 1))
}

# Generate rows for each outcome
row_vload <- get_diagnostic_row(bayes_vload, "Viral Load")
row_cd4   <- get_diagnostic_row(bayes_CD4,   "CD4 Count")
row_phys  <- get_diagnostic_row(bayes_phys,  "Physical QOL")
row_ment  <- get_diagnostic_row(bayes_ment,  "Mental QOL")

# Combine all rows into one large vertical plot
combined_diagnostics <- grid.arrange(
  row_vload, row_cd4, row_phys, row_ment, 
  ncol = 1,
  top = grid::textGrob("MCMC Diagnostics Across All Treatment Outcomes\n", 
                       gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold")),
  bottom = grid::textGrob("Figure 1: Assessing chain convergence (Trace), independence (ACF), and posterior consistency (Density)", 
                          gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 12, fontitalic = TRUE))
)
```


## Tables

```{r}
get_mcmc_summary <- function(model, outcome_name) {
  # Extract diagnostic fixed effects for 'hard_drugs'
  s <- summarise_draws(as_draws_df(model))
  
  diag <- rhat(model)[ "b_hard_drugs" ]
  ess_b <- s$ess_bulk[s$variable == "b_hard_drugs"]
  ess_t <- s$ess_tail[s$variable == "b_hard_drugs"]
  
  tibble(
    Outcome = outcome_name,
    Parameter = "Hard Drug Use",
    Rhat = round(diag, 3),
    ESS_Bulk = round(ess_b, 0),
    ESS_Tail = round(ess_t, 0)
  )
}

# Create the diagnostic table
mcmc_summary_table <- bind_rows(
  get_mcmc_summary(bayes_vload, "Viral Load"),
  get_mcmc_summary(bayes_CD4, "CD4 Count"),
  get_mcmc_summary(bayes_phys, "Physical QOL"),
  get_mcmc_summary(bayes_ment, "Mental QOL")
)

# Render with Flextable
ft_mcmc <- flextable(mcmc_summary_table) %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Rhat = "R-hat",
    ESS_Bulk = "Bulk ESS",
    ESS_Tail = "Tail ESS"
  ) %>%
  set_caption("Table 5: MCMC Convergence Diagnostics for Primary Predictor") %>%
  autofit() %>%
  theme_booktabs()
  

ft_mcmc
```

# Assess adherence 

```{r}
# FREQUENTIST MEDIATION CHECK
# Model adjusting for adherence
# ---------------------------------------------------------
adh_vload <- lm(log_vload.y ~ ADH + hard_drugs + log_vload.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE, 
                       data = hiv_merge)

adh_CD4 <- lm(LEU3N.y ~ ADH + hard_drugs + LEU3N.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE, 
                       data = hiv_merge)

adh_phys <- lm(AGG_PHYS.y ~ ADH + hard_drugs + AGG_PHYS.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE, 
                       data = hiv_merge)

adh_ment <- lm(AGG_MENT.y ~ ADH + hard_drugs + AGG_MENT.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE, 
                       data = hiv_merge)

# BAYESIAN MEDIATION MODEL
# We model both the adherence and the viral load simultaneously
# ---------------------------------------------------------
## Viral load
adh_bayes_vload <- brm(
  formula = log_vload.y ~ ADH + hard_drugs + log_vload.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE,
  data = hiv_merge,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "b"),      # Coefficients
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "sigma", lb = 0)    # Error term
  ),
  chains = 4, iter = 10000, warmup = 2000, core = 4, seed = 123
)

## CD4 count
adh_bayes_CD4 <- brm(
  formula = LEU3N.y ~ ADH + hard_drugs + LEU3N.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE,
  data = hiv_merge,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "b"),      # Coefficients
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "sigma", lb = 0)    # Error term
  ),
  chains = 4, iter = 10000, warmup = 2000, core = 4, seed = 123
)

## Physical QOL
adh_bayes_phys <- brm(
  formula = AGG_PHYS.y ~ ADH + hard_drugs + AGG_PHYS.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE,
  data = hiv_merge,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "b"),      # Coefficients
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "sigma", lb = 0)    # Error term
  ),
  chains = 4, iter = 10000, warmup = 2000, core = 4, seed = 123
)

## Mental QOL
adh_bayes_ment <- brm(
  formula = AGG_MENT.y ~ ADH + hard_drugs + AGG_MENT.x + 
                       age + BMI + RACE + EDUCBAS + SMOKE,
  data = hiv_merge,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "b"),      # Coefficients
    prior(normal(0, 10000), class = "sigma", lb = 0)    # Error term
  ),
  chains = 4, iter = 10000, warmup = 2000, core = 4, seed = 123
)
```

## Extract drug use effect from lm(with ADH)

```{r}
# Function to extract ADH effect
extract_freq_adh <- function(model, name, exp = FALSE) {
  res <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) %>% filter(term == "hard_drugs")
  
  est <- res$estimate
  low <- res$conf.low
  high <- res$conf.high
  
  if(exp == TRUE) {
    est <- 10^est; low <- 10^low; high <- 10^high
  }
  
  tibble(
    Outcome = name,
    `Estimate (non-Bayes)` = round(est, 3),
    SD = round(res$std.error, 3),
    `95% CI` = paste0("[", round(low, 2), ", ", round(high, 2), "]"),
    `p-value` = format.pval(res$p.value, eps = 0.001, digits = 3)
  )
}

```

## Extract drug use effect from Bayesian model(with ADH)

```{r}
extract_bayes_comparison <- function(model_no_adh, model_with_adh, name, exp = FALSE) {
  
    post <- as_draws_df(model_with_adh)$`b_hard_drugs`
    hpdi_obj <- bayestestR::hdi(post, ci = 0.95)
    waic_no <- waic(model_no_adh)$estimates["waic", "Estimate"]
    waic_with <- waic(model_with_adh)$estimates["waic", "Estimate"]
    
    est <- as.data.frame(posterior_summary(model_with_adh, variable = "b_hard_drugs"))$Estimate
    low <- hpdi_obj$CI_low
    high <- hpdi_obj$CI_high
    
    if(exp == TRUE) {
      est <- 10^est; low <- 10^as.numeric(low); high <- 10^as.numeric(high)
    }
    
    tibble(
      Model = name,
      `Estimate (Bayes)` = round(est, 3),
      SD = round(sd(post), 3),
      HPDI = paste0("[", round(low, 2), ", ", round(high, 2), "]"),
      `WAIC (no ADH)` = round(waic_no, 1),
      `WAIC (with ADH)` = round(waic_with, 1),
      `WAIC diff` = round(waic_with-waic_no, 1)
    )
  }

# Summary Table
freq_adh_summary <- bind_rows(
  extract_freq_adh(adh_vload, "Viral Load (Exp10)", exp = TRUE),
  extract_freq_adh(adh_CD4, "CD4 Count"),
  extract_freq_adh(adh_phys, "Physical QOL"),
  extract_freq_adh(adh_ment, "Mental QOL")
)

bayesian_adh_comp <- bind_rows(
  extract_bayes_comparison(bayes_vload, adh_bayes_vload, "Viral Load (Exp10)", TRUE),
  extract_bayes_comparison(bayes_CD4, adh_bayes_CD4, "CD4 Count", FALSE),
  extract_bayes_comparison(bayes_phys, adh_bayes_phys, "Physical QOL", FALSE),
  extract_bayes_comparison(bayes_ment, adh_bayes_ment, "Mental QOL", FALSE)
)

adh_sum <- bind_cols(freq_adh_summary, bayesian_adh_comp)

flextable(adh_sum) %>% 
  set_caption("Table 4: Frequentist and Bayesian Impact of Hard Drug Use on Outcomes Adjusting for Adherence (ADH)") %>%
  autofit() %>% theme_booktabs()
```

## Examine convergence
```{r}
# Plots
get_diagnostic_row(adh_bayes_vload, "Viral Load")
get_diagnostic_row(adh_bayes_CD4,   "CD4 Count")
get_diagnostic_row(adh_bayes_phys,  "Physical QOL")
get_diagnostic_row(adh_bayes_ment,  "Mental QOL")

# Create the diagnostic table
bind_rows(
  get_mcmc_summary(adh_bayes_vload, "Viral Load"),
  get_mcmc_summary(adh_bayes_CD4, "CD4 Count"),
  get_mcmc_summary(adh_bayes_phys, "Physical QOL"),
  get_mcmc_summary(adh_bayes_ment, "Mental QOL")
)
```


\pagebreak

## Code Appendix

```{r ref.label=knitr::all_labels(appendix == TRUE), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE, include=TRUE}
```
